rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative oxysulfonylation of o-vinyl benzoic acids with sulfonyl hydrazides to form γ-sulfonylated phthalide lactones
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of sulfonyl hydrazide to sulfonyl radical (via diazo intermediate, loss of N2); second anodic oxidation of benzylic C-radical adduct to a benzylic carbocation; cathodic reduction of protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: Benzylic carbon radical C from sulfonyl radical addition to o-vinyl benzoic acid
Benzylic carbocation D (after anodic oxidation of C)
γ-Sulfonylated phthalide product 3 (after intramolecular carboxylate cyclization and deprotonation)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes sulfonyl hydrazide (−3e−, −3H+) to a diazo species that extrudes N2 to give a sulfonyl radical; this radical adds to the vinyl group of the o-vinyl benzoic acid to form a benzylic C-radical, which is further oxidized at the anode to a benzylic cation that is captured intramolecularly by the ortho-carboxyl oxygen, with cathodic H+ reduction to H2 closing the circuit.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-Diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace 3a formed; radical trapping adduct 5 (Ph2C=CH-Ts type) detected by LC-MS, supporting a sulfonyl radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: Triethyl phosphite P(OEt)3
probe_reagents.observation: Yield of 3a reduced from 96% to 66%, consistent with a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: No-current control
probe_reagents.observation: No product 3a formed without electric current, confirming electrochemical activation is essential

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a and 2a
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peaks at 1.78 V (1a) and 1.39 V (2a) vs Ag/AgCl, indicating sulfonyl hydrazide 2a is preferentially oxidized at the anode to generate the sulfonyl radical

notes: Mechanism is consistent with literature precedent (refs 18–20). Radical–polar crossover (oxidation of C-radical to cation D) is invoked rather than direct radical cyclization; ortho-substituted substrates (4n, 4o) failed, attributed to steric hindrance rather than electronic effects.
